title: createEffect
description: Creates a reactive side effect that runs when its dependencies change. Use for side effects (logging, external calls). Runs after the current batch.
signature: createEffect<Next, Init = Next>(fn: (v: Init | Next) => Next, value?: Init, options?: EffectOptions): void
param_fn_desc: Effect function. Receives previous return value. Automatically tracked.
param_value_desc: Optional initial value passed to the first run.
usage_durable_outputs: The durable counter logs every tick. The effect reads <0>counter.count()</0>, so it re-runs each time the handler persists a new value, including the restored count after a restart.
usage_publish: The uptime monitor rewrites <0>./out/status.html</0> whenever the overall status or any table row changes. <1>createEffect</1> tracks both reads and re-runs the publish after each sweep.
info_batching: Effects are batched. Multiple synchronous signal updates only trigger one re-run. Use <0>batch()</0> explicitly for complex update sequences.
in_the_wild: See these effects in the <0>durable-counter</0> and <1>uptime-monitor</1> walkthroughs.
